title: Migrate the navigation sidebar (SidebarTree) to shadcn/ui
description: Migrate `SidebarTree` (sections → pages → tabs tree, inline create/rename, reorder buttons, delete confirmation) from inline styles to Tailwind + shadcn/ui, replacing the `▸`/`▾`/`✓`/`✕`/`↑`/`↓` text glyphs with lucide-react icons, without changing any interaction, tRPC call, or the TASK-67/70 behaviors (primary tab hidden from the tree, optional-slug create form).
acceptanceCriteria: Tree rows (sections, pages, tabs), the inline rename form, the create forms (`InlineCreate`, `CreatePageForm`), and the reorder ↑/↓ buttons are rebuilt with shadcn `Button`/`Input` + Tailwind layout, with lucide-react icons replacing the emoji/glyph carets and action buttons

All existing behaviors are unchanged: expand/collapse lazy-loads children, inline rename, reorder via ↑/↓, delete via confirmation (window.confirm or a shadcn `AlertDialog` — either is acceptable, but the destructive-cascade warning text must be preserved), the optional-slug hint from TASK-70, and the primary tab staying invisible in the tab list (TASK-67)
No change to any tRPC call or query invalidation logic
Verified in the browser: create/rename/reorder/delete at each level (section/page/tab), confirm the primary tab never appears, confirm blank-slug page creation still derives the slug

steps.details: Rebuild `NodeRow` and the section/page/tab row components with Tailwind layout + shadcn `Button` for the icon actions (expand caret, rename, delete, move up/down), using lucide-react icons (`ChevronRight`/`ChevronDown`, `Pencil`, `Trash2`, `ArrowUp`/`ArrowDown`) in place of the current text glyphs.
Rebuild `InlineCreate` and `CreatePageForm` with shadcn `Input`/`Button`, preserving the optional-slug hint text (TASK-70) and the inline error display on `CONFLICT`/`BAD_REQUEST`.

Optionally replace `window.confirm` cascade-delete warnings with a shadcn `AlertDialog` for a more polished confirmation — keep the exact warning copy (mentions what cascades) if doing so; this is a judgment call, not mandatory.
Exercise create/rename/reorder/delete at each tree level; confirm the primary tab is never listed (TASK-67); confirm creating a page with a blank slug still derives one from the title (TASK-70).

technicalNotes: This is the densest, most interaction-heavy screen in the admin — budget more care here than in TASK-77/80. Regression risk is highest for the lazy-load-on-expand and reorder logic; don't refactor that logic while restyling, only the presentation layer.
